layout_dets.text: ABSTRACT

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Currently, psychiatric practice lacks reliable predictive tools and a sufficiently detailed

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: mechanistic understanding of suicidal thoughts and behaviors (STB) to provide timely

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and personalized interventions. Developing computational models of STB that integrate

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: across behavioral, cognitive and neural levels of analysis could help better understand

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: STB vulnerabilities and guide personalized interventions. To that end, we present a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: computational model based on the active inference framework. With this model, we

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: show that several STB risk markers - hopelessness, Pavlovian bias and active-escape

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: bias - are interrelated via the drive to maximize one's model evidence. We propose four

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ways in which these effects can arise: (1) increased learning from aversive outcomes, (2)

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: reduced belief decay in response to unexpected outcomes, (3) increased stress sensitivity

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and () reduced sense of stressor controllability. These proposals stem from considering

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the neurocircuits implicated in STB: how the locus coeruleus - norepinephrine (LC-NE)

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: system together with the amygdala (Amy), the dorsal prefrontal cortex (dPFC) and the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: anterior cingulate cortex (ACC) mediate learning in response to acute stress and volatility

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: as well as how the dorsal raphe nucleus - serotonin (DRN-5-HT) system together with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the ventromedial prefrontal cortex (vmPFC) mediate stress reactivity based on perceived

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: stressor controllability. We validate the model by simulating performance in an Avoid/

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Escape Go/No-Go task replicating recent behavioral findings. This serves as a proof of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: concept and provides a computational hypothesis space that can be tested empirically

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and be used to distinguish planful versus impulsive STB subtypes. We discuss the relevance

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of the proposed model for treatment response prediction, including pharmacotherapy

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and psychotherapy, as well as sex differences as it relates to stress reactivity and suicide

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: risk.

layout_dets.text: 1 INTRODUCTION

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Karvelis and Diaconescu

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Computational Psychiatry

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Suicide is the second leading cause of death among young adults and among the top ten causes

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of death across all ages worldwide (Naghavi et al., 2017). Despite decades of research seeking to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: identiy risk factors of suicidal thoughts and behaviors (STB), their predictive ability remains lmited

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Large et al. 2016; Franklin et al., 2017). Some of the main risk factors include the following: prior

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: psychiatric diagnosis, treatment history, family history of psychopathology, prior self-injurious

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: thoughts and behaviors, substance use and psychosocial stress. However, multivariate suicide

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: risk models based on these factors do not have sufficient sensitivity and specificity in predicting

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: suicide and, even more importantly, lack mechanistic insight to offer clinically useful guidance

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: o selecting optimal individualized interventions (Kessler et al., 2020). As a result, current clinical

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: practice is in need of objective and reliable measures of suicide risk to not have to rely on self-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: reports, with ~50% of adults not disclosing their suicidal thoughts and remaining invisible for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: suicide prevention efforts (Mérelle et al., 2018).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: In recent years, cognitive theories have proposed several explanations for the progression from

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: emotional distress to suicidal ideation, and to suicide attempts (Van Orden et al., 2010; Klonsky

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and May, 2015; O'Connor and Kirtley, 2018; Bryan et al., 2020). At the core of these proposals

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: is the recognition that suicide can be viewed as a means to escape mental pain (psychache)

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Baumeister, 1990; Verrocchio et al., 2016). While mental pain and hopelessness contribute to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sical ideation, othe factors, collectively termed acquired capability for suicide' e.g., increasd

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: physical pain tolerance, access to lethal means), mediate the transition from ideation to suicide

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: attempt (for a review see Klonsky et al. (2018)). While providing useful high-level insights into

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the different psychological and environmental factors associated with suicidality, the verbal

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: nature of these theories limits their predictive power (Millner et al., 2020; Meehl, 1990). Natural

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: language is inherently vague resulting in intercorrelated constructs on which the theories rest,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: making it difficult to corroborate or refute them (Millner et al., 2020; Meehl, 1990). This calls for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: formal theories of suicidality which can be expressed computationally and which can define these

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: constructs operationally (Millner et al., 2020; Dombrovski and Hallquist, 2021). Computational

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: models could allow for a quantification of suicide risk and offer a more mechanistic insight for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: developing personalized clinical interventions (Nair et al., 2020; Millner et al., 2020). Just as

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: importantly, computational models can help bridge different levels of analysis and establish

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: mechanistic links between behavioral, cognitive, neural and even genetic variables, offering a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: more integrated understanding of the factors underlying vulnerability to STB (Huys et al., 2021).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: One principled way of building such models is to investigate vulnerability to STB through the lens of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: normative theories of learning and decision making in computational neuroscience (Dombrovski

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and Hallquist, 2021, 2017). Collectively, STB has been associated with deficits in cognitive control

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Richard-Devantoy et al. 2014) and impaired probabilistic learning in the context of rewards and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: punishments, including impaired delay discounting (Bridge et al., 2015), impaired reversal learning

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Dombrovski et al., 2010) and impaired value comparison during the choice process (Dombrovski

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: et al., 2019); for recent reviews see Lalovic et al. (2022) and Sastre-Buades et al. (2021). Outside

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of the laboratory, this is corroborated by findings of heightened suicide risk in gambling disorders

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Karlsson and Håkansson, 2018; Jolly et al., 2021). Behavioral insensitivity to adverse consequences

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and heightened sensitivity to internal emotional states have also been linked to suicide attempts

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Szanto et al., 2014). Together, these findings have led to a proposal of increased Pavlovian over

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: instrumental control as being an important contributing factor to vulnerability to STB (Dombrovski

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and Hallquist, 2017, 2021). The Pavlovian controller rigidly specifies stimulus-response mappings

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: regardless of outcomes, such as actively escaping proximal threats and avoiding distal threats,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: resulting in a rather reflexive behavior. In contrast, the instrumental control specifies stimulus-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: action-outcome mappings enabling one to adapt behaviors to environmental contingencies and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: maximize desired outcomes, which can be thought of as goal-directed behavior. In line with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the idea of increased Pavlovian biases, a recent study by Millner et al. (2019), found STB to be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: associated with an increased active-escape bias in an Avoid/Escape Go/No-Go task with aversive

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sound stimuli. In this study, the STB group was more biased towards choosing an active (Go)

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Importantly, these proposals stem from the consideration of neurocircuits implicated in STB.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Research on suicide neuromarkers point to the circuits underlying stress response, implicating

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the locus coeruleus - norepinephrine (LC-NE) and the dorsal raphe nucleus - serotonin (DRN-5-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: HT) systems (Mann and Rizk, 2020; Oquendo et al., 2014; van Heeringen and Mann, 2014). More

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: broadly, neuroimaging findings are converging on fronto-limbic regions involved in emotion

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: regulation and cognitive control, including the amygdala (Amy), the anterior cingulate cortex

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (ACC), the dorsal prefrontal cortex (dPFC) and the ventromedial prefrontal cortex (vmPFC) among

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: other regions (Schmaal et al., 2020; Balcioglu and Kose, 2018). However, computational models

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: linking these neuromarkers with the behavioral markers are stl missing. Here we suggest that our

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: proposed computational perturbations in STB could be related to how the LC-NE together with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the Amy, the dPFC and the ACC mediate learning in response to acute stress and volatility as well

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: as how the DRN-5-HT together with the vmPFC regulate stress responses based on the perceived

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: controllability of the aversive stimulus (Figure 1b).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: response in the presence of an aversive sound (in Escape condition), even when withholding the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: response (in No-Go condition) was the correct response.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Here, we aim to extend these ideas by proposing a computational mechanism for how the increased

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Pavlovian biases in STB could result from impaired probabilistic learning (Figure 1a). Importantly,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: we show how this is mediated by hopelessness (a belief that there is nothing one can do to make

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: things better), which is one of the most robust factors of suicide risk (Isometsä, 2014; May et al,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2020). To this end, we apply active inference as the most general neurocomputationally-principled

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: framework that integrates perception, action and learning into a continuous loop of information

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: processing (Friston et al., 2013). The principle guiding this information processing is maximization

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of (Bayesian) model evidence for one's model of the world, which simultaneously reduces

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: uncertainty about the world and achieves desired outcomes. We show that by operationalizing

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: hopelessness as predominantly negative instrumental beliefs (i.e., with all available actions

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: believed to have low probability of leading to the desired states), an increased Pavlovian control

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: emerges as a straightforward consequence of the drive to maximize model evidence. We

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: propose four different perturbations that within the context of aversive learning could give rise to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: hopelessness itsel: ()an increased learning from aversive outcomes, () a reduced belief decay

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: in response to unexpected outcomes, (3) an increased stress sensitivity and (4) a reduced sense

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of stressor controllability.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: To validate our model, we run model simulations in a probabilistic Avoid/Escape Go/No-Go task,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: demonstrating how the proposed perturbations lead to hopelessness, increased Pavlovian control

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and increased active-escape bias  replicating recent empirical findings by Millner et al. (2019).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: This serves as a proof of concept and produces a computational hypothesis space which can be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: investigated experimentally and which might speak to different subtypes of suicidal behaviour:

layout_dets.text: 2.1.1 Behavioral control

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2.1 MODELLING RATIONALE

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Karvelis and Diaconescu

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Computational Psychiatry

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Within the active inference framework, Pavlovian and instrumental modes of behavior can be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: derived from the same central computational goal, which could be thought of as maximizing

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: model evidence, resisting entropy or maintaining homeostasis (Pezzulo et al., 2015). Being nested

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: hierarchically from reflexive to Pavlovian, to habitual, to instrumental behaviorsdiferent modes

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of behavior allow for the successful navigation of increasingly more complex environments, but

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: also require more computational and metabolic resources. This poses a problem of bounded

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: rationality (i.e. finding a balance between behavioral accuracy and metabolic costs), which can

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: be resolved by performing Bayesian model averaging (BMA) over the different modes of behavior

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (FitzGerald et al., 2014). This means that actions are informed by all modes of behavior, whereby

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the modes with the highest model evidence have the most influence. In these computational

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: terms, a stronger active-escape bias in suicidality can be understood as resulting from a reduced

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: model evidence for instrumental relative to Pavlovian control.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: impulsive versus planful attempts (Schmaal et al., 2020; Dombrovski and Hallquist, 2017; Bernanke

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: While the learning rate can be affected by multiple neuromodulatory systems, when it comes

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: to adjusting the learning rate in response to acute stress and volatility, the LC-NE system plays a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: central role (Pulcu and Browning, 2019; Cook et al., 2019; Silvetti et al., 2018; Jepma et al., 2016;

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Lawson et al., 2020). Previous influential theories of LC function were founded on the assumption

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that LC-NE cells behave homogeneously (Yu and Dayan, 2005; Bouret and Sara, 2005). However,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: recent research emphasizes that LC firing properties are not topographically homogeneous and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: rather that the LC is comprised of largely non-overlapping target-specific subpopulations of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: neurons (Poe et al. 2020; Chandler et al., 2019). Importantly, aversive learning is mediated by

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Amy-LC connectivity (Sterpenich et al., 2006; Uematsu et al., 2017; Jacobs et al., 2020), whereas

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: connectivity between the prefrontal cortex (PFC) regions and the LC has been found to represent

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 'unlearning', which is necessary for faster adaptation to environmental change or volatility

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Uematsu et al., 2017; Sales et al., 2019). Relevant for our aims here, dPFC-LC connectivity has

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: been shown to encode learning from unpredictable feedback (Clewett et al., 2014) and response

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: conflict resolution (Köhler et al., 2016; Grueschow et al., 2020). The dorsolateral PFC (dIPFC) itself

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: has been associated with state prediction error (as opposed to reward prediction error) (Gläscher

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2 MATERIALS AND METHODS

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: In active inference, the model evidence of different policies (e.g., Pavlovian vs. instrumental)

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: depends on how well they are expected to result in desired outcomes (Friston et al., 2016).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Thus, saying that instrumental control has a reduced model evidence is the same as saying that

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: instrumental control is expected to have a reduced probability of fulfilling desired outcomes -i.e.,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: beliefs are more 'negative', not mathematically (not below zero), but colloquially speaking. Here

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: we operationalize hopelessness, which is one of the most robust suicide risk factors (May et al,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2020; Isometsä, 2014), as strong negative instrumental beliefs about state transitions.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2.1.2 Learning: uncertainty, stress and norepinephrine

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: To understand how hopelessness arises, we have to consider the dynamics of belief updating, i.e.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: learning. Having predominantly negative beliefs (hopelessness) implies either a predominantly

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: aversive environment or preferential learning from aversive events. Asymmetries in how positive

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and negative outcomes drive learning (i.e. affective bias) have been implicated in mood disorders

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Pulcu and Browning, 2017; Clark et al., 2018; Pulcu and Browning, 2019), with negative outcomes

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: having larger effect on learning than positive outcomes (Mathews and MacLeod, 2005; Eshel and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Roiser, 2010). Conversely, in the general population learning is driven more strongly by positive

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: outcomes (Sharot and Garrett, 2016). In STB, research on learning from negative vs. positive

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: outcomes is scarce, but a recent study showed STB to be associated with faster processing of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: negative stimuli (Harfmann et al., 2019).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Karvelis and Diaconescu

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Computational Psychiatry

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Recent work has shown that controllability of action outcomes governs arbitration between

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Pavlovian and instrumental control in line with BMA (Dorfman and Gershman, 2019). These effects

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: were found to be associated with frontal midline theta power, which suggests involvement of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the mPFC and the ACC (Csifcsák et al., 2020). Furthermore, it has been proposed that dorsal ACC

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (dACC) could be understood as encoding the expected value of control (Shenhav et al., 2013). This

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: is very similar to what we have proposed in relation to hopelessness. Indeed, controllability and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: hopelessness are very closely related constructs. Uncontrollable aversive stimulation has been

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: used to study learned helplessness, from which the construct of hopelessness has been derived

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Liu et al., 2015). Another extensively studied effect of controllability is that of modulating the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: stress response. Stressor controllability has been associated with the vmPFC-DRN-Amy network,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and thus with 5-HT-modulated stress response (Maier and Seligman, 2016; Kerr et al., 2012;

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Hiser and Koenigs, 2018). More specifically, stressor controllability activates the vmPFC, which

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: then inhibits DRN, which in turn reduces amygdala activation in response to a stressor (Maier and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Seligman, 2016). Relevant for our aims here, recent studies also show this effect to be associated

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: with successful instrumental learning (Collins et al., 2014; Wanke and Schwabe, 2020).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: et al., 2010). LC projections to the ACC have been shown to mediate updates of action-dependent

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: beliefs about the environment (Tervo et al., 2014; Sales et al., 2019), with the ACC encoding such

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: beliefs (Akam et al., 2021; Holroyd and Yeung, 2012). This is consistent with the findings that

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ACC activity correlates with reward expectation, prediction errors, learning rate and volatility

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Rushworth and Behrens, 2008), with these learning variables engaging the ACC primarily in the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: context of learning about the value of instrumental actions (Matsumoto et al., 2007).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Considering these findings, we introduce a computational distinction between hopelessness and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: controllability. As we have defined it earlier, hopelessness corresponds to negative instrumental

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: state-action beliefs that are encoded in the ACC and are arrived at via LC-mediated updates.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Controllability, on the other hand, we associate with the vmPFC-DRN-Amy network and thus

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: with 5-HT-modulated stress response. Instrumental state-transition beliefs encoded in the ACC

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Here, we propose that a disruption in any part of the Amy-dPFC-LC-ACC network (Figure 1b, blue)

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: could lead to hopelessness, increased Pavlovian and active-escape bias, increasing the risk of STB.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Specifically, we consider two possible perturbations. First, an increased Amy response to negative

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: outcomes would increase learning from negative outcomes (i.e., negative affective bias), which

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: may lead to more negative beliefs (hopelessness) and thus stronger Pavlovian influences. This is

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: supported by increased learning rate in STB observed in an aversive learning task (Millner et al.,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2019). Second, reduced activity in the dPFC in response to state-action prediction errors would

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: result in less belief decay allowing negative experiences to accumulate, thus also resulting in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: hopelessness and stronger Pavlovian biases. Interestingly, impairments in the dPFC have been

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: mostly associated with planful suicides (Schmaal et al., 2020), which would be in agreement with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the cognitive rigidity induced by reduced belief decay that we consider here.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Several lines of evidence suggest the aforementioned networks to be implicated in suicidality

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Schmaal et al., 2020; Oquendo et al., 2014). Studies have reported fewer LC neurons, LC

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: overactivity and depletion of NE, all of which are thought to be associated with a dysregulated

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: stress response (Oquendo et al. 2014; van Heeringen and Mann, 2014). The Amy is reported to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: show increased resting state functional connectivity (Kang et al., 2017) with some structural MRI

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: studies also reporting larger Amy volumes (Monkul et al., 2007; Spoletini et al., 2011). Studies on

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the dPFC report reduced volumes (Ding et al., 2015), decreased resting regional cerebral blood

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: flow (rCBF) (Willeumier et al., 2011) and reduced activation during error processing (Vanyukov

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: et al., 2016). ACC volumes are also reported to be reduced, with reductions in rostral ACC (rACC)

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: being most significant (Wagner et al., 2011). In a risk aversion task, suicide attempters showed a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: blunted subgenual ACC activation in response to potential gains (Baek et al., 2017), a reduced ACC

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: response to sad faces and an increased response to wins versus loses (Olié et al., 2015). Finally,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: a recent study found greater rACC-Amy functional connectivity to be associated with suicidal

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ideation and previous suicide attempts (Alarcón et al., 2019).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2.1.3 Controllability: stress and serotonin

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2.2 MODEL IMPLEMENTATION

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Karvelis and Diaconescu

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Computational Psychiatry

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Here we propose that a reduced sense of controllability stemming from vmPFC-DRN-Amy network

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: impairments (Figure 1b, pink) can lead to a stronger Amy activation in response to stress, thus

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: increasing learning from negative outcomes and leading to hopelessness and stronger Pavlovian

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: biases. Impairments in the vmPFC have been associated with impulsive suicide attempts (Schmaal

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: et al., 2020), which would be in line with larger belief updates in response to stressors.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: To model this task, we use an active inference scheme for discrete Markovian models (Friston et al.,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2016), which means that we will be dealing with discrete time steps (t), states (s), actions (a), and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: observations (o). Each trial gets divided into three time steps. At t = 1, the agent is in one of four

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: possible hidden states with no informative observations about the task conditions available (e.g., a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: fixation cross is displayed, which does not contain any information on which cue will be presented

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: next). At t = 2, the agent is presented with one of the four cues, which correspond to one of the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: four conditions resulting from the 2 x 2 (Go/No-Go x Avoid/Escape) factorial design. In the Avoid

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: In suicidality, a large body of research points to deficits in the serotonergic system (van Heeringen

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and Mann, 2014; Oquendo et al., 2014). While lower 5-hydroxyindoleacetic acid (5-HIAA) levels - a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: major serotonin metabolite - in the cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) suggest reduced overall serotonergic

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: activity (Mann et al., 2006), serotonin in the brainstem is found to be elevated (Bach et al., 2014),

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: with serotonergic action being elevated in the DRN due to less reuptake (Arango et al., 2001).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Furthermore, studies also report elevated serotonin binding in the Amy (Hrdina et al., 1993) and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: fewer serotonin transporters in the vmPFC and the ACC (Mann et al. 2000). A recent study has also

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: found a history of suicide attempts to be associated with a diminished functional connectivity

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: between vmPFC and Amy (Wang et al., 2020). Together, these findings are consistent with an

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: increased 5-HT-mediated stress response in suicidality.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The task employs a 2 × 2 (Go/No-go x Avoid/Escape) factorial design. On every trial, the agent is

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: presented with one of four cues. Two of the cues are always paired with an aversive sound (Escape

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: condition) while the other two are paired with silence (Avoid condition). The agent's goal is to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: learn, for each cue, which response (active Go or passive No-go) more frequently results in silence

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: during feedback. For ease of reference, we will refer to the responses that maximize the frequency

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of silence during the feedback as the "correct" responses throughout the paper. This means that

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: in the Avoid condition, correct responses will prevent the aversive sound from playing, while in the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the Escape condition, correct responses will stop the aversive sound that is already being played.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: However, the feedback is probabilistic, which means that even "correct" responses will sometimes

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: lead to experiencing the aversive sound. Probabilistic feedback introduces uncertainty and makes

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: it more challenging to learn, which response is correct.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: underlie inference about future states and future outcomes, which are then used to estimate

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: controllability in the vmPFC (see Model implementation section for more detailed rationale). This

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: provides a computational link between the NE-modulated and the 5-HT-modulated variables and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: allows hopelessness and controllability to be distinct but coupled. Interestingly, projections from

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the LC to the DRN have also been shown to regulate 5-HT release (Pudovkina et al., 2003) and be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: necessary for developing learned helplessness following uncontrollable stressor exposure (Grahn

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: et al., 2002), providing another point of interaction between the two neuromodulatory systems,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: which we do not specifically address here.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: In the previous sections we have laid out a conceptual pictureof our proposed model by considerig

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: various computational and neurobiological findings. In this section, we will present one possible

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: computational implementation by focusing on an Avoid/Escape Go/No-Go task (Figure 2a). Note

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that the implementation of the model is not at the level of neural dynamics but rather at the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: higher level of computational mechanisms underwritten by such dynamics (cf. Marr's levels of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: analysis (Marr and Poggio, 1976)). However, the active inference framework has deep connections

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: to neurobiology and has recently been applied to understanding a whole range of psychiatric

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: conditions (Smith et al., 2021), including the effects of noradrenergic and serotonergic drugs in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: depression (Constant et al., 2021).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Karvelis and Diaconescu

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: In the Escape condition, the aversive sound is present throughout the decision phase. At t = 3, the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: agent observes the final outcome of a trial, either aversive or neutral. This means that in the Avoid

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: condition, a correct action leads to no aversive sound, while in the Escape condition, a correct

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: action results in the discontinuation of the aversive sound. When choosing an action at t = 2, the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: agent relies on available policies : instrumental Go/No-Go and Pavlovian. Probabilities of these

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: probabilities of policies depend on model evidence that each set of beliefs provides, where model

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: evidence is approximated with variational free energy (see S1 Appendix: full mathematical

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: details of the model and Da Costa et al. (2020) for more details).

layout_dets.text: the agent has the information to update their beliefs about state transitions. To account for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: instrumental learning facilitated by Pavlovian responses (Holmes et al., 2010), one could consider

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: combining the posterior probabilities of Pavlovian Go or No-Go responses with instrumental Go and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: No-Go policy probabilities, respectively, when updating beliefs about controlled state transitions.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: However, in the simulations presented in this paper, Pavlovian effects are never too extreme and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: similar results can be obtained with either implementation. To keep the model simpler, here we

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: present the results using the original implementation where the sum in the second term of the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: equation is performed only over the two instrumental policies.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The remaining two parameters n and λ in Eq. (1) control the learning rate and the decay rate,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: respectively.Thelearning rate controls how much new experiencesadd to the existing concentration

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: parameters, while the decay rate controls how much the previously accumulated concentration

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: parameters should be discounted. Without the decay factor, concentration parameters would

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: accumulate indefinitely making the agent too rigid and thus too slow to adapt if environmental

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: contingencies were to change. Following the work of Sales et al. (2019), λ is assumed to depend

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: on state-action prediction errors (SAPEs) and to be associated with effective connectivity from

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the dPFC to the LC. This makes the decay factor sensitive to environmental volatility: changing

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: environmental contingencies will result in larger SAPEs, which in turn will speed up unlearning of no

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: longer accurate beliefs, allowing the agent to learn the new contingencies faster. The relationship

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: between SAPEs and λ is modelled using a logistic function:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: value and is more active during both outcome and decision phases (cf. instrumental control and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: learning) (Vassena et al., 2014). The close relationship between the subjective feeling of control

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and outcome valuation has also been demonstrated in recent studies (Stolz et al., 2020; Wang

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and Delgado, 2019). Relevantly, STB has been associated with reduced activation to expected

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: value in vmPFC (Brown et al., 2020; Dombrovski and Hallquist, 2017).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: In addition to being sensitive to environmental change (i.e. volatility), the LC-NE system also

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: coordinates aversive learning mediated by Amy-LC connectivity (Uematsu et al., 2017; Jacobs et

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: al. 2020). To capture these effects, we introduce a learning rate dependency on outcome valence

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (assuming Amy activation during aversive outcomes), which we associate with the preference

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: against aversive outcomes encoded in the C vector:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: aversive stimulus outcomes and 0 for the neutral outcomes. Parameter k is a scaling factor that

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: rate dependence on valence that we introduce here is what enables the model to account for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2010). A more principled implementation of valence and its role in modulating the learning rate

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: could depend on the rate of change of free energy over time (Joffily and Coricelli, 2013).

layout_dets.text: 3.1 MODEL SIMULATIONS

layout_dets.text: 3 RESULTS

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: For the initial simulations (Figures 4 and 5 ), we ran 200 trials of the task, where at every trial one of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the 4 cues was presented at random. After 100 trials, the meanings of the cues were reversed: Go

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: becoming No-Go and vice versa. In this simulation, the model parameters were set to k = 0.1, m

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and β = 1 to produce reasonable performance

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: trajectories as well as an active-escape bias (Figure 4a) consistent with empirical findings reported

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: by Millner et al. (2018). As the agent's beliefs approach the actual state transition probabilities

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Figure f-i colored lines), this makes the neutral outcomesmore expected, thus invoking only small

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: SAPEs in contrast to unexpected aversive outcomes (Figure 4d). This is also what drives successful

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: unlearning after the reversal: a series of negative outcomes with large SAPEs result in a sharp drop

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: in the decay parameter (Figure 4e, black line), which increases belief decay and facilitates quick

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: learning of new contingencies. The Pavlovian policy that underlies the active-escape bias can be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: seen at its strongest at the very beginning of the task and right after the reversal, when belifs that

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: instrumental actions will lead to neutral outcomes are lower (Figure 4f-i).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Figure 3 summarizes the proposed computations as well as their possible neural correlates and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: highlights parameters of interest for STB.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Note that even though we have provided a reasonable theoretical justification for introducing the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: controllability component, t is a rather ad hoc addition to the otherwise computationally principled

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: active inference framework. It is important to stress, however, that the simulation results that we

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: present in the next section do not hinge on this additional computation, except for the results

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: concerning the controllability parameter itself.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: To validate the model, we first simulated performance on the task for a single healthy control

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Figure 4), and then showed how increasing parameter k - which regulates aversiveness-related

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: component of the learning rate and is assumably represented in terms of Amy-LC connectivity -

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: can produce increased active-escape biases and other behavioral and cognitive aspects associated

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: with suicidality (Figure 5). Finally, we defined a wider hypothesis space, exploring how different

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: parameters in the model can independently lead to the behavior observed in STB. (Figure 6).

layout_dets.text: 3.2 MULTIPLE ROUTES TO AN ACTIVE-ESCAPE BIAS

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: While directly increasing learning from aversive outcomes (k) is one way to produce the effects

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: associated with STB, there is a wider hypothesis space to be explored. To that end, we performed

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: a more extensive investigation of the effects of other model parameters. In this context, it is

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: important to note that the model exhibits a considerable degree of stochasticity when initiated

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: with the chosen parameter configurations and thus, the results presented earlier in Figures 4 and 5

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: are meant to be primarily llustrative. To reduce stochasticityand to obtain more robust behavioral

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: results, now we used 400 trials with a reversal at 200 and ran 50 simulations for each parameter

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: configuration. To visualize the results, we computed relevant task performance summary statistics

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (mean and standard error) for each parameter configuration (Figure 6). The first column in Figure

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 6 simply reproduces the results in Figures 4 and 5, showing that as we increase learning from

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: negative outcomes, we reduce beliefs that instrumental actions will lead to the desired states

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (igure 6a), which leads to an increasein the probability of the Pavlovian policy Figure ), whichi

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: turn leads to a larger active-escape bias (Figure 6i). As a result of the increased biases, we also see

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: a slight decrease in the overall performance accuracy (Figure 6m).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Reducing base belief decay (increasing parameter m) produces similar results of more negative

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: belies, a higher probability of the Pavlovian policy and a stronger active-escape bias(Figuref).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: parameter c's contribution to the learning rate. Interestingly, both reduced and increased distress

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tolerance have been associated with suicide risk: lower distress tolerance relates to psychological/

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: social pain and contributes to suicidal ideation, while higher distress tolerance relates to physical

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: pain and contributes to the acquired capability for suicide (see Liu et al. (2016) for a discussion).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Our model simulations are agnostic to the nature of the aversive stimulus used and thus might be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: capturing both of these effects.

layout_dets.text: 4 DISCUSSION

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: In this paper, we presented a computational model of hopelessness and Pavlovian/active-escape

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: bias in suicidality. With this model we showed that increased Pavlovian control and active-escape

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: biases result from state hopelessness via the drive to maximize model evidence. Moreover, we

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: proposed how hopelessness itself can arise from four mechanisms: (1) increased learning from

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: aversive outcomes, (2) reduced belief decay in response to unexpected outcomes, (3) increased

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: stress sensitivity and (4) reduced sense of stressor controllability. We also proposed how these

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: alterations might relate to the neurocircuits implicated in suicidality. Specifically, we considered

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: perturbations in the LC-NE system together with the Amy, the dPFC and the ACC, which mediate

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: learning in response to acute stress and volatility, as well as perturbations in the DRN-5-HT system

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: together with the vmPFC and the Amy, which regulate stress reactivity and its modulation by

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: perceived controllability. We validated the model via simulations of an Avoid/Escape Go/No-Go task

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: reproducing the active-escape biases reported by Millner and colleagues (Millner et al., 2019, 2018).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: First, it is worthwhile to elaborate on what advantages and new insights our proposed model

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: brings compared to previous modelling work. Millner et al. (2019) analyzed the increased active-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: escape bias in STB using a combined reinforcement learning - drift diffusion model (RL-DDM) and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: found that an increased active-escape bias can be explained by a bias parameter (aka a starting

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: point in the DDM part of the model). This parameter was assumed to be constant throughout the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: task. In contrast, our proposed model offers a mechanistic explanation for how active-escape bias

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: arises dynamically from learning about the state transition probabilities and balancing between

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: instrumental and Pavlovian policies. Unlike in RL-DDM, in our model, Pavlovian and instrumental

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: pcrerepresenteplicitly.Imprantly,hialweustoreate statetransitin proa

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tostate hopelessness (which is a central construct in suicidality research (Klonsky et al. 2018; May

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: et al., 2020; Isometsä, 2014)), offering a possible operationalization of the hopelessness construct.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Finally, using the active inference framework enabled us to propose several links (some more

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: speculative than others) between the model variables and the underlying neurocircuitry, which

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: could help bridge the explanatory gap between neurobiology and cognition in STB (see Limitations

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: section for further discussion).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Our model simulation results offer a computational hypothesis space by identifying mechanistically

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: distinct perturbations that lead to hopelessness and Pavlovian/active-escape biases associated with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: STB. These distinct pathways might also speak to different suicidality subtypes: impulsive versus

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: planful (Schmaal et al. 2020; Bernanke et al., 2017). While all of the four parameter manipulations

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: produced increased Pavlovian control and active-escape biases, examining the trajectories of belief

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: updating revealed that reduced belief decay led to more gradual updates and more stable negative

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: beliefs as well as more stable and elevated Pavlovian influences, which could be associated with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: gradual progression towards more negative beliefs and an increased influence of the Pavlovian

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: policy (Figure 7a). For high

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (low controllability), high k (high stress weight) and high c (high stress

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sensitivity), we see increasingly larger and sudden spikes in Pavlovian biases. Considering the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: influence of the Pavlovian policy as a proxy for STB risk, the former scenario suggests a constantly

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: increasing risk of STB and thus could be related to planful suicide attempts, while the latter scenao

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: suggests an increased STB risk immediately after the occurrence ofaversive events and could relate

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: to impulsive suicide attempts. Bearing in mind our proposed links between the model parameters

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and the underlying neurocircuitry (Figure 3), these results are consistent with planful and impulsive

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: suicide attempt subtypes, with the former being predominantly associated with dPFC activity and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the latter being predominantly associated with vmPFC activity (Schmaal et al., 2020).

layout_dets.text: Karvelis and Diaconescu

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: While throughout the paper we have adopted a transdiagnostic view of STB, many mental

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: disorders are known to increase suicide risk. Among all disorders, borderline personality disorder

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (BPD), depression, bipolar disorder, schizophrenia, and anorexia nervosa show the highest risk

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of suicide - between 10 to 45 times higher than the general population (Chesney et al., 2014).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Comorbidities further increase suicide risk by inflicting higher levels of distress (Nock et al., 2010;

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Jylhä et al., 2016), with the majority of suicides being estimated to occur within a major depressive

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: episode (Isometsä, 2014). Recent studies show preliminary evidence that suicide subtypes might

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: cut across the current categories of disorders, with higher suicidal ideation variability (i.e. higher

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: stress responsiveness) being associated with childhood physical abuse, aggression, and impulsivity

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: in major depressive disorder (Oquendo et al., 2020) and with affective lability in BDP (Rizk et al.,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2019). In a similar way, we might expect that the ways in which different mental disorders increase

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the risk of suicide could also map onto the different ways in which the effects associated with STB

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: can emerge within our proposed model.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: When it comes to pharmacotherapy, sub-anesthetic doses of ketamine, a N-methyl-D-aspartate

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: receptor (NMDAR) antagonist, is currently oneofthemost promisinginterventions for rapid reduction

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of STB, but only 55-60% of individuals respond with a complete remission (Wilkinson et al. 2018).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The exact mechanism through which ketamine achieves its anti-suicidal and anti-depressant

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: effects is still not fully understood (Riggs and Gould, 2021). Many hypotheses emphasize the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: importance of increased α-amino-3-hydroxy-5-methyl-4-isoxazolepropionic acid receptor (AMPAR)

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: signalling, its involvement in bottom-up information transmission and a consequent increase in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: synaptic and spine plasticity (Zanos and Gould, 2018; Lengvenyte et al., 2019). Other recent in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: vivo microdialysis findings suggest ketamine-induced AMPAR signaling in LC and DRN as well as a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: subsequent release of NE and 5-HT in the mPFC to be necessary for the rapid antidepressant effects

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (López-Gil et al., 2019; Llamosas et al., 2019; Pham et al., 2017), also implicating prelimbic cortex

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (a homolog to Brodmann's area 32 in the vmPFC) (PL)-DRN involvement in stressor controllability

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Amat et al., 2016; Dolzani et al., 2018). A recent review also highlights the ACC to be playing a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: more planful STB. The other three manipulations - reduced controllability of stressors, increased

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: learning from aversive outcomes and increased stress sensitivity - resulted in increasingly variable

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: belief updates with sudden spikes in Pavlovian biases after aversive outcomes, which could be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: associated with more impulsive STB. Considering the dPFC and the vmPFC as possible correlates of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: beli decay and controllability and its effects on stress reactivity), respectively, our results are n

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: agreement with neuroimaging studies associating disruptions in vmPFC activity with the impulsive

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: STB subtype and the dPFC activity with the planful STB subtype (Schmaal et al., 2020).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Beingable to stratifythe propensity for suicidal bhavio into mechanisticallydistinc subgroups could

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: help improve early interventions and treatment response prediction. Many different psychotherapies

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ar applied in the context of suicidality, including the manualized therapies such as CBT, Dialectical

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Behavior Therapy (DTB), and mentalization-based therapy (MTB). However, evidence for the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: effectiveness of different psychotherapies is still scarce and it remains unclear which components of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: W

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: et al., 2010). Moreover, the attempts to determine these unknowns are likely complicated by not

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: accounting for the etiological heterogeneity in high suicide risk groups (Iyengar et al., 2018). Current

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: neurobiological models of the mechanism of action of psychotherapy point to neural substrates of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: executive and semantic processes and highlight the vmPFC and its involvement in implicit emotion

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: regulation as well as dPFC and its involvement in explicit behavioral control (Messina et al., 2016).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: This would map to the stressor controllability (vmPFC) and belief decay (dPFC) components in our

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: proposed model and would suggest these parameters to be relevant when assessing, monitoring or

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: optimizing the effectiveness of psychotherapy for a given suicidality subtype. For example, we could

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: think of the conrollability parameteras reflecting the level of felt control over one'sinner and outer

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: life whereas the belief decay parameter could capture one's ability to unlearn maladaptive beliefs

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: through new experiences, behavior or cognitive reappraisal (Zilverstand et al., 2017).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 4.1 MODEL-BASED SUICIDALITY SUBTYPES AND PERSONALIZED

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: INTERVENTIONS

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Karvelis and Diaconescu

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Computational Psychiatry

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 4.2 LIMITATIONS

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: While we have considered some of the most crucial neurocircuits and neuromodulatory systems

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: at the overlap of stress response, aversive learning, behavioral control and STB, there remain other

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: relevant regions to be considered (Schmaal et al., 2020; Lengvenyte et al., 2019). Of particular

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: importance may be the lateral habenula (LHb), an epithalamic nucleus acting as a relay hub between

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: forebrain and midbrain structures and playing a significant role in learning from non-rewarding and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: aversive experiences (Matsumoto and Hikosaka, 2009). The LHb is involved in stressor controllability

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: effects via the DRN-5-HT system (Metzger et al., 2017) and is one of the locations targeted by

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ketamine that mediates the anti-depressant effects (Zanos and Gould, 2018; Yang et al., 2018a;

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Shepard et al. 2018). LHb activity has been associated with depressive symptoms of helplessness,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: anhedonia, and excessive negative focus (Yang et al., 2018b), while a recent study also reported

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: higher resting state functional connectivity between LHb and several brain regions, including the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: amygdala, to be associated with STB independently of depressive symptoms (Ambrosi et al. 2019).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: It is worth emphasizing that while a close consideration of the networks implicated in STB informed

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the construction of the model proposed here, the implementation of the model is not at the level of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: neural dynamics but rather at the level of higher-order computational mechanisms underwritten

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: by such dynamics (cf. Marr's levels of analysis (Marr and Poggio, 1976). This means that the model

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: variables might not necessarily neatly map onto distinct elements of the neurocircuitry but might

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: interact with several other factors. For example, while we regard parameter c in the prior over

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: outcomes to correspond to stress sensitivity and Amy activation, we could imagine other factors

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: contributing to dispreference of the aversive outcome beyond its aversiveness per se, such as

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: contextual factors relating to task engagement and a general motivation to do well in the task.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Similarly, controllability threshold,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: might reflect a combined influence of changes in vmPFC

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: activation, its connectivity to the DRN, connectivity from the DRN to the Amy or even the LHb

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and the effects it exerts on the DRN-5-HT system. Future work, including modelling of the neural

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: dynamics and gathering empirical data, will help clarify these relationships.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Personalization of early interventions could also be improved by a more mechanistic understanding

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of sex differences as it relates to STB (Williams and Trainor, 2018). Females show a higher incidence

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of suicidal intent and suicide attempts, although the rate of completed suicides is much higher

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: in males (2 to 5 times) (Freeman et al., 2017). Suicide risk factors have also been found to differ

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: between the sexes (Oquendo et al., 2007). While multiple psychosocial factors are likely to be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: contributing to these differences (Canetto and Sakinofsky, 1998), sexual dimorphisms in the brain

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: might play an important role as well (Pallayova et al., 2019). For example, structural and functional

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: dimorphisms in the LC-NE system and its regulation by estrogen in females is associated with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: an increased susceptibility to hyperarousal (Bangasser et al., 2016), which itself has been linked

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: to a higher risk of suicidal ideation (Steyn et al., 2013; Morabito et al., 2020; Dolsen et al., 2017).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Preclinical studies also suggest important sex differences in how stressor controllability modulates

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: stress reactivity. Unlike males, females do not seem to benefit from increased controllability,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: with the lack of engagement and structural plasticity within the PL-DRN pathway being a likely

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: mechanism for these differences (Fallon et al. 2020). The model proposed here might help better

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: understand how these differences impact stress reactivity and controllability, and how this affects

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: response to ketamine as well as to other interventions (Fallon et al., 2020).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Related to the above limitations, it is also important to reiterate that the hypothesis space

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: presented in this paper serves as a proof of concept and is not intended to be exhaustive. The

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: emergence of STB risk factors in different contexts is most likely to involve other variables that

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: we have not yet considered. Furthermore, our simulations explored only the simplest scenarios

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of varying one parameter at a time. Considering how these parameters interact provides another

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: layer of complexity. For example, we could expect different subtypes of STB to be related not to a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: single parameter, but to a unique combination of multiple parameters, forming distinct clusters

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: key role in mediating ketamine's antidepressant effects (Alexander et al., 2021). The model we

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: introduced here could help provide a more mechanistic understanding of how the changes in belief

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: updating and possibly activity in these brain regions relate to reduced suicide risk.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The additional file for this article can be found as follows:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Karvelis and Diaconescu

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Computational Psychiatry

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Finally, it also important to point out that most of the model parameters that we focused on

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: in this paper are not unique to the active inference framework. To explain the active-escape

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: bias phenomenon, we had to introduce additional parameters and computations. Namely, we

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: introduced the dependency of the learning rate on outcome values and the dependency of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: outcome values on controllability, with controllability being anotheraddition in itself. Such a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: modelling approach where additional parameters and computations are added on top of the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: existing ones is not uncommon and has been applied in many reinforcement learning modelling

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: approaches focused on Pavlovian and instrumental control mechanisms (e.g., Dorfman and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Gershman, 2019; Guitart-Masip et al., 2012; Mkrtchian et al., 2017; Na et al., 2021; Grossman et

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: al., 2021). However, it contrasts with most of the developments in active inference, where model

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: extensions are derived from the free energy functional. While we justified the introduction of these

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: computations by relying on a large body of literature investigating the mechanisms of interest, a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: more principled derivation of computations capturing these mechanisms might be possible.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: within the multidimensional parameter space. Future work with empirical data will allow for the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: further refinement of the hypothesis space and the delineation of different STB subtypes.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: We acknowledge the generous support from the Krembil Foundation. We are also thankful to Dr.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Venkat Bhat for clinical feedback and to Colleen Charlton for stylistic suggestions.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Furthermore, in the work presented here we have not explicitly addressed the distinction between

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: suicide ideators and suicide attempters. Recent accounts of suicidality argue that suicidal ideation

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and the progression from ideation to attempts should be treated as separate processes (Van

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Orden et al., 2010; Klonsky and May, 2015; O'Connor and Kirtley, 2018; Bryan et al. 2020; Klonsky

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: et al., 2018). The active inference framework might be well suited to study these distinctions as it

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: explicitly models and factorizes inferences about the states of the world (cf. suicidal ideation) and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: action selection (cf. suicide attempt). This will be more thoroughly explored in future work.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Our proposed model and the insights it provides is also limited by the behavioral task to which

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the model was applied. In the task considered here, the stimulus is completely unambiguous

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and there is only one decision per trial to make. Notably, in the special case when outcomes

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: unambiguously specify hidden states, active inference reduces to a simpler KL-control model

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Friston et al., 2015), and is similar to model-based reinforcement learning models that are driven

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: by reward maximization (see Eq. (12) in S1 Appendix: full mathematical details of the model for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: more detailed explanation). Introducing sensory uncertainty and multiple decisions would allow

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: for the utilization of the unique aspects of active inference, namely epistemic action  a goal-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: directed sampling of information Friston et al. (2015). This would provide a more ecologically valid

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: context to study the relationship between information sampling dynamics and STB. Such tasks

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: would allow us to capture other phenomena relevant for STB, such as aversive generalization

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (how specificaversive events lead to negative belies about the world), its relationship to trauma,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: its effects on reduced problem-solving abilities (i.e. planning) and its influence on biases towards

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: escape strategies (Linson and Friston, 2019; Linson et al., 2020).
